items.headline: Thunder and Knicks on the Brink of Conference Finals!
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder (7-0 in playoffs) lead their Western Conference Semifinals series against the Los Angeles Lakers 3-0 after a dominant Game 3 victory. Meanwhile, the New York Knicks hold a commanding 3-0 lead over the Philadelphia 76ers in their Eastern Conference Semifinals matchup.

items.headline: Star Power in Trade Rumors: Brown, Murray, Irving, Davis Heating Up Offseason Discussions.
items.summary: With 22 teams already looking towards the offseason, trade rumors are swirling around prominent names like Jaylen Brown (BOS), Jamal Murray (DEN), Kyrie Irving (DAL), and Anthony Davis (WAS). The Portland Trail Blazers and Toronto Raptors are also reportedly eyeing big moves, including potential pursuits of Giannis Antetokounmpo.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Breakout Season for Blazers Signals Exciting Future.
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija wrapped up a stellar breakout season for the Portland Trail Blazers, averaging over 15 points, 7 rebounds, and 4 assists per game. His impressive play, including a 41-point, 12-assist performance in a Play-In game, has the Blazers looking to build around his aggressive, downhill style with more shooting.

items.headline: Mitchell, Harden Lead Cavs to Crucial Game 3 Victory
items.summary: Donovan Mitchell exploded for 35 points and James Harden added 19 points, including 9 clutch fourth-quarter points, as the Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 116-109 in Game 3. Detroit's Cade Cunningham recorded a triple-double with 27 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists, but also committed 8 turnovers. The Pistons now lead the series 2-1.

items.headline: Thunder Dominate Lakers in Game 3, Take Commanding 3-0 Series Lead
items.summary: Ajay Mitchell delivered career playoff highs with 24 points and 10 assists without a single turnover, alongside Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 23 points and 9 assists, as the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled past the Los Angeles Lakers 131-108 in Game 3. The Thunder now hold a formidable 3-0 lead in the series, with the Lakers still without an injured Luka Doncic.

items.headline: Knicks Eye Sweep Against 76ers in Game 4
items.summary: The New York Knicks look to close out their Eastern Conference Semifinals series against the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 4. Joel Embiid (PHI) is probable to play despite right hip soreness, while OG Anunoby (NYK) is questionable with a hamstring strain.

items.headline: Spurs Host Timberwolves in Pivotal Game 4
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs will host the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 4 of their Western Conference Semifinals series. The Spurs lead the series 2-1 after a strong Game 3 performance.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Reaches Postseason Milestone
items.statContext: Led Cavaliers to Game 3 victory over Pistons, reaching 2,000 career postseason points (9th fastest in NBA history).

items.headline: Ajay Mitchell Shines for Thunder in Game 3
items.statContext: Recorded career playoff highs in points and assists with no turnovers, stepping up in the absence of Jalen Williams.

items.headline: Cunningham Posts Triple-Double in Loss
items.statContext: Achieved his second career postseason triple-double against the Cavaliers, though accompanied by 8 turnovers.

items.headline: Wemby's Monster Performance in Game 3
items.statContext: Dominated in Game 3 win over the Timberwolves with a massive stat line.

items.headline: Key Injury Updates: Doncic Out, Embiid Probable
items.summary: Lakers star Luka Doncic remains sidelined week-to-week with a hamstring injury, while Thunder's Jalen Williams is also out. Joel Embiid is probable for the 76ers' critical Game 4 today with right hip soreness, and OG Anunoby (NYK) is questionable.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Semifinals Update
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons lead the Cleveland Cavaliers 2-1 after the Cavs' Game 3 victory. The New York Knicks are one win away from the Eastern Conference Finals, holding a 3-0 series lead over the Philadelphia 76ers ahead of Game 4.

items.headline: Western Conference Semifinals Update
items.summary: The defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder are on the verge of sweeping the Los Angeles Lakers, leading their series 3-0. In the other Western Conference matchup, the San Antonio Spurs lead the Minnesota Timberwolves 2-1.

items.headline: Offseason Trade Market Eyeing Big Names
items.summary: Beyond the active playoff teams, several stars are being discussed in early offseason trade rumors, including Jaylen Brown, Jamal Murray, Kyrie Irving, and Anthony Davis. The Blazers and Raptors are also exploring blockbuster deals, with Giannis Antetokounmpo emerging as a potential target.
